course.title: Advanced Git 2.55: Internals, Recovery, Scale, and Production Workflows
course.description: Developers, maintainers, release engineers, and incident responders who already use Git confidently and need production-grade repository reasoning. This original, research-backed path uses repeated retrieval, cumulative artifacts, changed-case labs, stakeholder projects, and a performance exam.
outcomes: Demonstrate advanced git 2.55 reference and object model, revision algebra, storage, reflogs, recovery, layered undo, merge topology, conflict stages, rerere, rewriting, replay, bisect, worktrees, refspecs, maintainer workflows, signatures, submodules, partial clone, sparse checkout, attributes, filters, trust, maintenance, bundles, sha-256 transition, and disaster defense through an independently verified stakeholder artifact.

Demonstrate safe stateful repository simulation with changed graphs, exact commands, observable state transitions, causal diagnosis, and recovery verification through an independently verified stakeholder artifact.
Demonstrate five cumulative production repository projects and a performance-based certification examination through an independently verified stakeholder artifact.
sharedRequirements: Every activity retrieves Git Foundations safety, worktree/index/ref/graph distinctions, accessibility, security, testing, and evidence habits before adding one advanced boundary.
All browser Git work is deterministic simulation only. No learner command reaches host Git, files outside the scenario, credentials, hooks, network remotes, or executable repository content.

Passing work requires exact before and after refs, graph and tree evidence, changed-case behavior, failure diagnosis, recovery anchor, residual risk, and live-environment transfer limits appropriate to the competency.

competencies.statement: Distinguish direct refs, symbolic refs, pseudorefs, remote-tracking refs, packed refs, and detached HEAD using plumbing evidence.
misconceptions: Every ref is a loose text file under .git/refs.
masteryEvidence: A changed authentic task independently demonstrates this outcome: Distinguish direct refs, symbolic refs, pseudorefs, remote-tracking refs, packed refs, and detached HEAD using plumbing evidence.

competencies.statement: Resolve revision names and disambiguate refs, object IDs, paths, reflog selectors, and peel operators before an operation.
misconceptions: A short token is interpreted as a branch whenever a matching branch exists.
masteryEvidence: A changed authentic task independently demonstrates this outcome: Resolve revision names and disambiguate refs, object IDs, paths, reflog selectors, and peel operators before an operation.

competencies.statement: Construct inclusive, exclusive, symmetric-difference, ancestry, parent, and path-limited revision sets for an exact question.
misconceptions: A..B and A...B select the same commits and differ only in patch formatting.
masteryEvidence: A changed authentic task independently demonstrates this outcome: Construct inclusive, exclusive, symmetric-difference, ancestry, parent, and path-limited revision sets for an exact question.

competencies.statement: Use parent selection, first-parent history, generation, and merge-base evidence without confusing display order with causality.
misconceptions: The first commit printed by log is necessarily the direct cause of the observed change.
masteryEvidence: A changed authentic task independently demonstrates this outcome: Use parent selection, first-parent history, generation, and merge-base evidence without confusing display order with causality.

competencies.statement: Capture repository boundaries, object format, refs, worktrees, index state, operation state, and recovery anchors before investigation.
misconceptions: A clean working tree is a complete forensic baseline.
masteryEvidence: A changed authentic task independently demonstrates this outcome: Capture repository boundaries, object format, refs, worktrees, index state, operation state, and recovery anchors before investigation.

competencies.statement: Trace blobs, trees, commits, annotated tags, parent edges, and reference roots as an immutable reachable object graph.
misconceptions: A commit contains file contents directly instead of naming a root tree.
masteryEvidence: A changed authentic task independently demonstrates this outcome: Trace blobs, trees, commits, annotated tags, parent edges, and reference roots as an immutable reachable object graph.

competencies.statement: Explain loose objects, pack indexes, delta bases, multi-pack indexes, and why storage representation does not change object identity.
misconceptions: A deltified object has an ID derived from its delta instructions.
masteryEvidence: A changed authentic task independently demonstrates this outcome: Explain loose objects, pack indexes, delta bases, multi-pack indexes, and why storage representation does not change object identity.

competencies.statement: Distinguish reachable, unreachable, dangling, promised, missing, and corrupt objects using fsck and reference evidence.
misconceptions: Every dangling object is corrupt and should be deleted immediately.
masteryEvidence: A changed authentic task independently demonstrates this outcome: Distinguish reachable, unreachable, dangling, promised, missing, and corrupt objects using fsck and reference evidence.

competencies.statement: Use rev-parse, cat-file, verify-pack, count-objects, and batch inspection to answer bounded questions without checkout or execution.
misconceptions: git show is the safest and complete inspector for every object and storage problem.
masteryEvidence: A changed authentic task independently demonstrates this outcome: Use rev-parse, cat-file, verify-pack, count-objects, and batch inspection to answer bounded questions without checkout or execution.

competencies.statement: Quarantine suspect storage, preserve alternate copies and refs, verify object connectivity, and defer destructive pruning until recovery proof exists.
misconceptions: Running gc is an appropriate first repair for a missing-object report.
masteryEvidence: A changed authentic task independently demonstrates this outcome: Quarantine suspect storage, preserve alternate copies and refs, verify object connectivity, and defer destructive pruning until recovery proof exists.

competencies.statement: Explain per-ref and HEAD reflogs, selector syntax, creation policy, worktree scope, expiration categories, and configuration.
misconceptions: Reflogs are shared remotely and provide a permanent repository audit log.
masteryEvidence: A changed authentic task independently demonstrates this outcome: Explain per-ref and HEAD reflogs, selector syntax, creation policy, worktree scope, expiration categories, and configuration.

competencies.statement: Correlate ref updates, checkout history, operation messages, timestamps, actors, and graph evidence without treating reflog order as commit ancestry.
misconceptions: HEAD@{1} always means the parent of the current commit.
masteryEvidence: A changed authentic task independently demonstrates this outcome: Correlate ref updates, checkout history, operation messages, timestamps, actors, and graph evidence without treating reflog order as commit ancestry.

competencies.statement: Discover lost tips with reflog, fsck, object inspection, and known content while avoiding commands that may expire or prune candidates.
misconceptions: Running gc first makes lost commits easier to discover because it organizes every object.
masteryEvidence: A changed authentic task independently demonstrates this outcome: Discover lost tips with reflog, fsck, object inspection, and known content while avoiding commands that may expire or prune candidates.

competencies.statement: Create a protective ref, inspect recovered tree and history, compare intended content, and validate changed behavior before integration.
misconceptions: Finding the old object ID completes recovery even when its tree and lineage are unverified.
masteryEvidence: A changed authentic task independently demonstrates this outcome: Create a protective ref, inspect recovered tree and history, compare intended content, and validate changed behavior before integration.

competencies.statement: Design reflog expiration, gc grace, backup, and incident policy from collaboration and compliance needs without promising archival durability.
misconceptions: Increasing reflog expiry converts local Git storage into a verified backup.
masteryEvidence: A changed authentic task independently demonstrates this outcome: Design reflog expiration, gc grace, backup, and incident policy from collaboration and compliance needs without promising archival durability.

competencies.statement: Classify the affected worktree, index, ref, graph, published history, and deployed-state layers before selecting an undo operation.
misconceptions: Undo is one Git operation whose only variable is the target commit.
masteryEvidence: A changed authentic task independently demonstrates this outcome: Classify the affected worktree, index, ref, graph, published history, and deployed-state layers before selecting an undo operation.

competencies.statement: Use restore source, staged, worktree, pathspec, patch, and conflict-stage controls while preserving unrelated changes.
misconceptions: git restore always changes HEAD together with the selected file content.
masteryEvidence: A changed authentic task independently demonstrates this outcome: Use restore source, staged, worktree, pathspec, patch, and conflict-stage controls while preserving unrelated changes.

competencies.statement: Predict soft, mixed, hard, merge, keep, path-limited, and intent-to-add reset effects on HEAD, index, and worktree.
misconceptions: git reset --hard affects only tracked files and therefore cannot destroy important work.
masteryEvidence: A changed authentic task independently demonstrates this outcome: Predict soft, mixed, hard, merge, keep, path-limited, and intent-to-add reset effects on HEAD, index, and worktree.

competencies.statement: Revert ordinary commits, ranges, and merges with explicit mainline parent, sequencer handling, semantic tests, and publication evidence.
misconceptions: Reverting a merge makes Git forget that the merged branch was ever integrated.
masteryEvidence: A changed authentic task independently demonstrates this outcome: Revert ordinary commits, ranges, and merges with explicit mainline parent, sequencer handling, semantic tests, and publication evidence.

competencies.statement: Preserve refs or patches, preview affected paths and commits, perform the smallest repair, and verify topology, content, behavior, and remote impact.
misconceptions: A successful exit status proves the undo restored the intended system behavior.
masteryEvidence: A changed authentic task independently demonstrates this outcome: Preserve refs or patches, preview affected paths and commits, perform the smallest repair, and verify topology, content, behavior, and remote impact.

competencies.statement: Compute one or multiple best common ancestors, octopus bases, fork points, and independent tips for complex graphs.
misconceptions: Every pair of commits has exactly one merge base.
masteryEvidence: A changed authentic task independently demonstrates this outcome: Compute one or multiple best common ancestors, octopus bases, fork points, and independent tips for complex graphs.

competencies.statement: Choose fast-forward, no-ff, ff-only, or refusal from release policy and graph evidence rather than branch-name folklore.
misconceptions: A fast-forward is determined by whether branch names share the same prefix.
masteryEvidence: A changed authentic task independently demonstrates this outcome: Choose fast-forward, no-ff, ff-only, or refusal from release policy and graph evidence rather than branch-name folklore.

competencies.statement: Evaluate ort strategy behavior and bounded options for renames, whitespace, subtree shifts, and favoring conflict hunks without replacing semantic review.
misconceptions: The ours strategy and the -X ours option preserve the same tree and history.
masteryEvidence: A changed authentic task independently demonstrates this outcome: Evaluate ort strategy behavior and bounded options for renames, whitespace, subtree shifts, and favoring conflict hunks without replacing semantic review.

competencies.statement: Recognize when octopus, subtree, or unrelated-history integration is appropriate and when staged pairwise review is safer.
misconceptions: Octopus merge is a general conflict-resolution tool for many active feature branches.
masteryEvidence: A changed authentic task independently demonstrates this outcome: Recognize when octopus, subtree, or unrelated-history integration is appropriate and when staged pairwise review is safer.

competencies.statement: Verify merge parents, combined diff, resulting tree, tests, release invariants, signatures, and rollback path before publication.
misconceptions: A conflict-free merge proves both sides remain behaviorally compatible.
masteryEvidence: A changed authentic task independently demonstrates this outcome: Verify merge parents, combined diff, resulting tree, tests, release invariants, signatures, and rollback path before publication.

competencies.statement: Inspect stage 1 base, stage 2 ours, stage 3 theirs, unmerged entries, and higher-order conflict metadata before editing.
misconceptions: Conflict markers contain all base, rename, mode, and stage evidence needed for resolution.
masteryEvidence: A changed authentic task independently demonstrates this outcome: Inspect stage 1 base, stage 2 ours, stage 3 theirs, unmerged entries, and higher-order conflict metadata before editing.

competencies.statement: Distinguish content, add/add, delete/modify, rename, directory/file, binary, mode, submodule, and recursive conflicts and select evidence for each.
misconceptions: Every conflict can be completely diagnosed by reading only textual conflict markers.
masteryEvidence: A changed authentic task independently demonstrates this outcome: Distinguish content, add/add, delete/modify, rename, directory/file, binary, mode, submodule, and recursive conflicts and select evidence for each.

competencies.statement: Reconstruct intent from base and both histories, produce a semantic combined result, and test invariants that neither side tested alone.
misconceptions: Removing all conflict markers proves the combined behavior preserves both sides.
masteryEvidence: A changed authentic task independently demonstrates this outcome: Reconstruct intent from base and both histories, produce a semantic combined result, and test invariants that neither side tested alone.

competencies.statement: Enable, inspect, forget, retrain, and validate rerere preimages and postimages while understanding automatic staging and retention.
misconceptions: Rerere records a universal correct answer keyed only by pathname.
masteryEvidence: A changed authentic task independently demonstrates this outcome: Enable, inspect, forget, retrain, and validate rerere preimages and postimages while understanding automatic staging and retention.

competencies.statement: Record conflict cause, chosen invariant, rerere provenance, changed-case tests, and abort or continuation evidence for future integrators.
misconceptions: Once rerere applies cleanly, human and behavioral review are redundant.
masteryEvidence: A changed authentic task independently demonstrates this outcome: Record conflict cause, chosen invariant, rerere provenance, changed-case tests, and abort or continuation evidence for future integrators.

competencies.statement: Model rebase as commit selection, temporary reset, ordered replay, new object creation, and ref movement rather than branch movement magic.
misconceptions: Rebase moves existing commit objects to new parents without changing their IDs.
masteryEvidence: A changed authentic task independently demonstrates this outcome: Model rebase as commit selection, temporary reset, ordered replay, new object creation, and ref movement rather than branch movement magic.

competencies.statement: Use --onto, upstream, branch, fork-point, and explicit revision sets to select exactly which commits move and where.
misconceptions: --onto selects every commit reachable from the branch, including the upstream history.
masteryEvidence: A changed authentic task independently demonstrates this outcome: Use --onto, upstream, branch, fork-point, and explicit revision sets to select exactly which commits move and where.

competencies.statement: Use pick, reword, edit, squash, fixup, exec, drop, break, label, reset, and merge actions while preserving dependency order.
misconceptions: Interactive rebase actions may be reordered freely because the final tree is all reviewers need.
masteryEvidence: A changed authentic task independently demonstrates this outcome: Use pick, reword, edit, squash, fixup, exec, drop, break, label, reset, and merge actions while preserving dependency order.

competencies.statement: Evaluate autosquash, rebase-merges, update-refs, autostash, empty-commit, and backend tradeoffs against collaboration policy.
misconceptions: Combining every convenience flag always produces the safest and most faithful rewrite.
masteryEvidence: A changed authentic task independently demonstrates this outcome: Evaluate autosquash, rebase-merges, update-refs, autostash, empty-commit, and backend tradeoffs against collaboration policy.

competencies.statement: Protect the old tip, inspect rewritten mapping, compare patch series and trees with range-diff, test changed behavior, and recover from abort or error.
misconceptions: Equal final trees prove a rewrite preserved reviewable intent and authorship.
masteryEvidence: A changed authentic task independently demonstrates this outcome: Protect the old tip, inspect rewritten mapping, compare patch series and trees with range-diff, test changed behavior, and recover from abort or error.

competencies.statement: Select and order commits for cherry-pick using explicit revision traversal while distinguishing argument order from revision-walk order.
misconceptions: Multiple cherry-pick arguments are always replayed in command-line order regardless of revision traversal.
masteryEvidence: A changed authentic task independently demonstrates this outcome: Select and order commits for cherry-pick using explicit revision traversal while distinguishing argument order from revision-walk order.

competencies.statement: Handle merge mainline, empty, redundant, no-commit, signoff, and conflict continuation choices with release-policy evidence.
misconceptions: --mainline chooses which branch name should receive the resulting commit.
masteryEvidence: A changed authentic task independently demonstrates this outcome: Handle merge mainline, empty, redundant, no-commit, signoff, and conflict continuation choices with release-policy evidence.

competencies.statement: Use patch-id, cherry marks, range-diff, and tree comparisons to detect likely equivalent changes while naming heuristic limits.
misconceptions: Matching commit messages or issue IDs prove two patches are equivalent.
masteryEvidence: A changed authentic task independently demonstrates this outcome: Use patch-id, cherry marks, range-diff, and tree comparisons to detect likely equivalent changes while naming heuristic limits.

competencies.statement: Explain current git replay behavior, ref-update output, supported options, and why experimental plumbing needs version-gated adoption.
misconceptions: git replay is a drop-in replacement for every interactive rebase workflow.
masteryEvidence: A changed authentic task independently demonstrates this outcome: Explain current git replay behavior, ref-update output, supported options, and why experimental plumbing needs version-gated adoption.

competencies.statement: Verify source provenance, target applicability, conflicts, changed-platform behavior, release notes, signatures, and rollback for each supported line.
misconceptions: A clean cherry-pick means the fix is complete on an older dependency and runtime stack.
masteryEvidence: A changed authentic task independently demonstrates this outcome: Verify source provenance, target applicability, conflicts, changed-platform behavior, release notes, signatures, and rollback for each supported line.

competencies.statement: Inspect stash commit structure, parents, refs, reflog, staged state, and untracked representation instead of treating a stash as an external bag.
misconceptions: Stashed changes live outside the object database and cannot affect retention.
masteryEvidence: A changed authentic task independently demonstrates this outcome: Inspect stash commit structure, parents, refs, reflog, staged state, and untracked representation instead of treating a stash as an external bag.

competencies.statement: Use pathspec, patch, staged, keep-index, include-untracked, and all controls while previewing secret and ignored-file consequences.
misconceptions: A path-limited stash cannot capture secrets because it does not create Git objects.
masteryEvidence: A changed authentic task independently demonstrates this outcome: Use pathspec, patch, staged, keep-index, include-untracked, and all controls while previewing secret and ignored-file consequences.

competencies.statement: Choose apply, pop, branch, store, create, export, or import from collision risk, portability, and retention needs.
misconceptions: pop is always preferable because successful cleanup proves the stash is no longer needed.
masteryEvidence: A changed authentic task independently demonstrates this outcome: Choose apply, pop, branch, store, create, export, or import from collision risk, portability, and retention needs.

competencies.statement: Compare stash, diff, format-patch/am, and bundle transfer by metadata, prerequisites, reviewability, trust, and connectivity.
misconceptions: A plain diff preserves authorship, commit messages, prerequisite history, and signatures exactly like a bundle.
masteryEvidence: A changed authentic task independently demonstrates this outcome: Compare stash, diff, format-patch/am, and bundle transfer by metadata, prerequisites, reviewability, trust, and connectivity.

competencies.statement: Create named durable refs or encrypted external backups for important work, verify restoration on a changed base, and expire temporary objects deliberately.
misconceptions: A stash is an appropriate durable backup and collaboration channel.
masteryEvidence: A changed authentic task independently demonstrates this outcome: Create named durable refs or encrypted external backups for important work, verify restoration on a changed base, and expire temporary objects deliberately.

competencies.statement: Explain binary search over candidate ancestry, good and bad polarity, convergence, and the assumptions that make a reported first bad commit meaningful.
misconceptions: Bisect tests commits in timestamp order and always needs exactly log2(n) trials.
masteryEvidence: A changed authentic task independently demonstrates this outcome: Explain binary search over candidate ancestry, good and bad polarity, convergence, and the assumptions that make a reported first bad commit meaningful.

competencies.statement: Define known good and bad endpoints, path limits, terms, first-parent choices, and ancestry validity before the first trial.
misconceptions: The bad endpoint should be the parent of the suspected change rather than a revision that demonstrates the failure.
masteryEvidence: A changed authentic task independently demonstrates this outcome: Define known good and bad endpoints, path limits, terms, first-parent choices, and ancestry validity before the first trial.

competencies.statement: Build a hermetic bisect run harness with explicit exit meanings, timeouts, cleanup, logs, deterministic fixtures, and no unsafe repository code execution.
misconceptions: Any nonzero test exit means bad, so setup failures and timeouts need no separate contract.
masteryEvidence: A changed authentic task independently demonstrates this outcome: Build a hermetic bisect run harness with explicit exit meanings, timeouts, cleanup, logs, deterministic fixtures, and no unsafe repository code execution.

competencies.statement: Handle untestable commits, flaky observations, merge topology, multiple introduction paths, and inconclusive adjacent candidates honestly.
misconceptions: Skipping a trial preserves ordinary binary-search certainty and still identifies one exact first bad commit.
masteryEvidence: A changed authentic task independently demonstrates this outcome: Handle untestable commits, flaky observations, merge topology, multiple introduction paths, and inconclusive adjacent candidates honestly.

competencies.statement: Reproduce the boundary, inspect the candidate diff and dependencies, revert or patch experimentally, retain a regression test, and distinguish correlation from cause.
misconceptions: The commit printed by bisect is automatically the root cause and its author owns the incident.
masteryEvidence: A changed authentic task independently demonstrates this outcome: Reproduce the boundary, inspect the candidate diff and dependencies, revert or patch experimentally, retain a regression test, and distinguish correlation from cause.

competencies.statement: Distinguish common repository data from per-worktree HEAD, index, refs, operation state, config, and administrative links.
misconceptions: Each linked worktree contains an independent clone and object database.
masteryEvidence: A changed authentic task independently demonstrates this outcome: Distinguish common repository data from per-worktree HEAD, index, refs, operation state, config, and administrative links.

competencies.statement: Add branch, detached, orphan, and relative-path worktrees; then lock, unlock, repair, and move them with path and device constraints.
misconceptions: Moving a linked worktree directory manually updates its administrative link automatically.
masteryEvidence: A changed authentic task independently demonstrates this outcome: Add branch, detached, orphan, and relative-path worktrees; then lock, unlock, repair, and move them with path and device constraints.

competencies.statement: Reason about checked-out branch exclusivity, shared refs, concurrent fetch or maintenance, and safe cross-worktree branch deletion and rewrite.
misconceptions: Linked worktrees isolate branch refs, so rewriting main in one cannot affect another.
masteryEvidence: A changed authentic task independently demonstrates this outcome: Reason about checked-out branch exclusivity, shared refs, concurrent fetch or maintenance, and safe cross-worktree branch deletion and rewrite.

competencies.statement: Identify stale administrative entries, missing paths, moved repositories, expiration, and prunable worktrees before repair or cleanup.
misconceptions: Every missing worktree directory is abandoned and safe to prune immediately.
masteryEvidence: A changed authentic task independently demonstrates this outcome: Identify stale administrative entries, missing paths, moved repositories, expiration, and prunable worktrees before repair or cleanup.

competencies.statement: Design named purpose, directory, branch, owner, lock, tool-cache, secret, cleanup, and handoff rules for parallel work.
misconceptions: Worktrees isolate hooks, config, ignored build outputs, and secrets by default.
masteryEvidence: A changed authentic task independently demonstrates this outcome: Design named purpose, directory, branch, owner, lock, tool-cache, secret, cleanup, and handoff rules for parallel work.

competencies.statement: Parse positive and negative fetch and push refspecs, wildcard mappings, force markers, deletion, matching, and configured defaults.
misconceptions: origin/main is a live alias that automatically follows the remote branch.
masteryEvidence: A changed authentic task independently demonstrates this outcome: Parse positive and negative fetch and push refspecs, wildcard mappings, force markers, deletion, matching, and configured defaults.

competencies.statement: Reason about advertised refs, negotiation, shallow or filtered boundaries, tag following, prune, prune-tags, and remote-tracking updates.
misconceptions: --prune deletes local branches and every tag absent from the remote.
masteryEvidence: A changed authentic task independently demonstrates this outcome: Reason about advertised refs, negotiation, shallow or filtered boundaries, tag following, prune, prune-tags, and remote-tracking updates.

competencies.statement: Predict fast-forward acceptance by namespace and object type and distinguish local preflight from server receive policy.
misconceptions: All namespaces use the same commit fast-forward rule and servers cannot impose stricter policy.
masteryEvidence: A changed authentic task independently demonstrates this outcome: Predict fast-forward acceptance by namespace and object type and distinguish local preflight from server receive policy.

competencies.statement: Use explicit force-with-lease expectations, force-if-includes, atomic push, dry-run, and signed-push evidence while accounting for background fetch.
misconceptions: Bare --force-with-lease is a permanent guarantee that unseen remote work cannot be lost.
masteryEvidence: A changed authentic task independently demonstrates this outcome: Use explicit force-with-lease expectations, force-if-includes, atomic push, dry-run, and signed-push evidence while accounting for background fetch.

competencies.statement: Capture remote URLs, authentication boundary, exact source and destination, expected old and new IDs, server result, and post-fetch verification.
misconceptions: A successful push proves every collaborator and deployment now observes the same ref.
masteryEvidence: A changed authentic task independently demonstrates this outcome: Capture remote URLs, authentication boundary, exact source and destination, expected old and new IDs, server result, and post-fetch verification.

competencies.statement: Represent dependent changes as explicit base and tip relationships, keep each review independently testable, and update downstream stacks after revision.
misconceptions: A stack is independently reviewable whenever its final tip builds, even if intermediate commits do not.
masteryEvidence: A changed authentic task independently demonstrates this outcome: Represent dependent changes as explicit base and tip relationships, keep each review independently testable, and update downstream stacks after revision.

competencies.statement: Use range-diff and interdiff-style evidence to explain rerolls, reordered commits, changed commit messages, and semantic deltas between series.
misconceptions: Range-diff proves semantic equivalence whenever old and new patches receive an equals marker.
masteryEvidence: A changed authentic task independently demonstrates this outcome: Use range-diff and interdiff-style evidence to explain rerolls, reordered commits, changed commit messages, and semantic deltas between series.

competencies.statement: Compare topic-branch, integration-branch, subsystem, patch-flow, merge-queue, and release-train workflows by authority and recovery cost.
misconceptions: A maintainer workflow is defined entirely by branch names rather than authority and evidence flow.
masteryEvidence: A changed authentic task independently demonstrates this outcome: Compare topic-branch, integration-branch, subsystem, patch-flow, merge-queue, and release-train workflows by authority and recovery cost.

competencies.statement: Model base freshness, batch size, speculative testing, queue invalidation, required evidence, and starvation without hiding the exact integrated revision.
misconceptions: Testing a pull request once keeps that evidence valid after its base and queue batch change.
masteryEvidence: A changed authentic task independently demonstrates this outcome: Model base freshness, batch size, speculative testing, queue invalidation, required evidence, and starvation without hiding the exact integrated revision.

competencies.statement: Define authorship, signoff, review, update, merge, rewrite, rollback, deletion, archival, and exception rules with observable enforcement.
misconceptions: One universal branching model is best for every project size, release cadence, and trust structure.
masteryEvidence: A changed authentic task independently demonstrates this outcome: Define authorship, signoff, review, update, merge, rewrite, rollback, deletion, archival, and exception rules with observable enforcement.

competencies.statement: Distinguish lightweight refs from annotated tag objects, nested tags, peeling, target types, messages, tagger identity, and replacement risk.
misconceptions: An annotated tag points directly to file content and cannot target another tag or non-commit object.
masteryEvidence: A changed authentic task independently demonstrates this outcome: Distinguish lightweight refs from annotated tag objects, nested tags, peeling, target types, messages, tagger identity, and replacement risk.

competencies.statement: Verify supported GPG, SSH, or X.509 signatures while separating cryptographic validity, key identity, trust policy, authorization, and time evidence.
misconceptions: A cryptographically valid signature proves the signer was authorized for that release.
masteryEvidence: A changed authentic task independently demonstrates this outcome: Verify supported GPG, SSH, or X.509 signatures while separating cryptographic validity, key identity, trust policy, authorization, and time evidence.

competencies.statement: Compare signed commits, tags, merges, and pushes by what is covered, who verifies them, and which server or client policy applies.
misconceptions: Signing a push retroactively signs every commit and tree reachable from the updated ref.
masteryEvidence: A changed authentic task independently demonstrates this outcome: Compare signed commits, tags, merges, and pushes by what is covered, who verifies them, and which server or client policy applies.

competencies.statement: Fetch and push tags explicitly, detect movement and namespace collisions, handle deletion, and apply immutable-release policy without assuming universal enforcement.
misconceptions: Tags fetched once are immutable locally and remotely unless the object database is corrupt.
masteryEvidence: A changed authentic task independently demonstrates this outcome: Fetch and push tags explicitly, detect movement and namespace collisions, handle deletion, and apply immutable-release policy without assuming universal enforcement.

competencies.statement: Bind reviewed source, verified tag target, clean reproducible build, artifact digest, attestation, publication record, and rollback ref.
misconceptions: A valid signed tag proves which artifact bytes users received and that the build was reproducible.
masteryEvidence: A changed authentic task independently demonstrates this outcome: Bind reviewed source, verified tag target, clean reproducible build, artifact digest, attestation, publication record, and rollback ref.

competencies.statement: Explain gitlink entries, superproject commits, submodule repositories, checked-out commits, .gitmodules, local config, and detached submodule HEAD.
misconceptions: A submodule stores another repository and its full branch state inside the superproject commit.
masteryEvidence: A changed authentic task independently demonstrates this outcome: Explain gitlink entries, superproject commits, submodule repositories, checked-out commits, .gitmodules, local config, and detached submodule HEAD.

competencies.statement: Predict init, update, sync, set-url, set-branch, recursive, remote, merge, rebase, and shallow behavior across configuration layers.
misconceptions: git submodule update --remote always records and publishes a new superproject gitlink automatically.
masteryEvidence: A changed authentic task independently demonstrates this outcome: Predict init, update, sync, set-url, set-branch, recursive, remote, merge, rebase, and shallow behavior across configuration layers.

competencies.statement: Stage gitlink changes, publish dependency commits before superproject refs, review nested diffs, and verify that every referenced object is reachable.
misconceptions: Publishing the superproject first guarantees the referenced submodule commit is available to every clone.
masteryEvidence: A changed authentic task independently demonstrates this outcome: Stage gitlink changes, publish dependency commits before superproject refs, review nested diffs, and verify that every referenced object is reachable.

competencies.statement: Treat URLs, protocols, hooks, recursive commands, credentials, relative paths, and untrusted nested code as explicit trust boundaries.
misconceptions: .gitmodules is inert data and cannot influence protocols, paths, credentials, or recursive execution.
masteryEvidence: A changed authentic task independently demonstrates this outcome: Treat URLs, protocols, hooks, recursive commands, credentials, relative paths, and untrusted nested code as explicit trust boundaries.

competencies.statement: Compare submodules with package managers, subtree merges, vendoring, monorepos, and build-time fetches by reproducibility, ownership, atomicity, and operations.
misconceptions: Submodules are always obsolete because package managers can represent every source dependency.
masteryEvidence: A changed authentic task independently demonstrates this outcome: Compare submodules with package managers, subtree merges, vendoring, monorepos, and build-time fetches by reproducibility, ownership, atomicity, and operations.

competencies.statement: Explain promisor remotes, promised objects, filter specifications, lazy fetching, missing-object tolerance, and command compatibility.
misconceptions: A blobless clone is shallow history with old commits removed.
masteryEvidence: A changed authentic task independently demonstrates this outcome: Explain promisor remotes, promised objects, filter specifications, lazy fetching, missing-object tolerance, and command compatibility.

competencies.statement: Compare shallow clone, partial clone, sparse checkout, sparse index, single branch, and reference repositories by omitted data and semantic limits.
misconceptions: Shallow, partial, and sparse clones omit the same data and differ only in command syntax.
masteryEvidence: A changed authentic task independently demonstrates this outcome: Compare shallow clone, partial clone, sparse checkout, sparse index, single branch, and reference repositories by omitted data and semantic limits.

competencies.statement: Use cone patterns, non-cone patterns, set, add, reapply, disable, skip-worktree state, and sparse index while detecting vivified paths.
misconceptions: Skip-worktree is a promise that Git will never materialize or inspect the path.
masteryEvidence: A changed authentic task independently demonstrates this outcome: Use cone patterns, non-cone patterns, set, add, reapply, disable, skip-worktree state, and sparse index while detecting vivified paths.

competencies.statement: Measure clone transfer, object count, index size, status latency, checkout, fetch, build input, cache, and developer task before choosing an optimization.
misconceptions: Repository size alone identifies the optimization and makes task-level measurements unnecessary.
masteryEvidence: A changed authentic task independently demonstrates this outcome: Measure clone transfer, object count, index size, status latency, checkout, fetch, build input, cache, and developer task before choosing an optimization.

competencies.statement: Verify required commands and tools against changed working sets, define full-object release gates, preserve an offline fallback, and document experimental limits.
misconceptions: If sparse status is fast, builds and releases necessarily have every required file and object.
masteryEvidence: A changed authentic task independently demonstrates this outcome: Verify required commands and tools against changed working sets, define full-object release gates, preserve an offline fallback, and document experimental limits.

competencies.statement: Resolve attributes from system, repository, info, directory, pattern, and negative states using check-attr evidence.
misconceptions: Attributes use the same pattern semantics and precedence as .gitignore.
masteryEvidence: A changed authentic task independently demonstrates this outcome: Resolve attributes from system, repository, info, directory, pattern, and negative states using check-attr evidence.

competencies.statement: Design text, eol, working-tree-encoding, binary, and renormalization policy with byte-level and cross-platform verification.
misconceptions: Setting text=auto rewrites all existing index entries and safely fixes line endings without review.
masteryEvidence: A changed authentic task independently demonstrates this outcome: Design text, eol, working-tree-encoding, binary, and renormalization policy with byte-level and cross-platform verification.

competencies.statement: Configure diff drivers, textconv, binary markers, merge drivers, recursive behavior, and conflict-marker size without treating presentation as storage.
misconceptions: A custom diff driver changes stored blob bytes and therefore defines merge correctness.
masteryEvidence: A changed authentic task independently demonstrates this outcome: Configure diff drivers, textconv, binary markers, merge drivers, recursive behavior, and conflict-marker size without treating presentation as storage.

competencies.statement: Threat-model clean, smudge, and process filters; define required-filter failure, idempotence, delayed output, secrets, and missing-tool behavior.
misconceptions: A clean filter may safely discard source because smudge can always reconstruct it.
masteryEvidence: A changed authentic task independently demonstrates this outcome: Threat-model clean, smudge, and process filters; define required-filter failure, idempotence, delayed output, secrets, and missing-tool behavior.

competencies.statement: Choose ordinary Git, LFS-style pointers, release artifacts, package registries, or external storage from review, locking, retention, availability, and migration needs.
misconceptions: Any large file automatically belongs in a pointer-based extension regardless of access and history needs.
masteryEvidence: A changed authentic task independently demonstrates this outcome: Choose ordinary Git, LFS-style pointers, release artifacts, package registries, or external storage from review, locking, retention, availability, and migration needs.

competencies.statement: Inspect configuration origin, scope, type, includes, conditional includes, worktree config, environment, and command overrides before trusting behavior.
misconceptions: git config --list reports values in decisive precedence order, so origin and scope are unnecessary.
masteryEvidence: A changed authentic task independently demonstrates this outcome: Inspect configuration origin, scope, type, includes, conditional includes, worktree config, environment, and command overrides before trusting behavior.

competencies.statement: Explain protected configuration and safe.directory ownership exceptions without using broad wildcards to conceal unsafe shared repositories.
misconceptions: safe.directory=* changes filesystem ownership and therefore repairs unsafe repository permissions.
masteryEvidence: A changed authentic task independently demonstrates this outcome: Explain protected configuration and safe.directory ownership exceptions without using broad wildcards to conceal unsafe shared repositories.

competencies.statement: Map client and server hook timing, working directory, environment, parameters, exit behavior, bypass paths, distribution, and enforcement limits.
misconceptions: A committed hooks directory creates an unavoidable server-enforced policy for every contributor.
masteryEvidence: A changed authentic task independently demonstrates this outcome: Map client and server hook timing, working directory, environment, parameters, exit behavior, bypass paths, distribution, and enforcement limits.

competencies.statement: Choose credential helpers, URL matching, useHttpPath, askpass, environment, token scope, and erasure behavior without writing secrets into repository state.
misconceptions: Credential helpers match only host names and never vary by protocol, username, path, or configured context.
masteryEvidence: A changed authentic task independently demonstrates this outcome: Choose credential helpers, URL matching, useHttpPath, askpass, environment, token scope, and erasure behavior without writing secrets into repository state.

competencies.statement: Inspect untrusted repositories as data in an isolated environment; disable execution paths, verify objects and config, and grant trust narrowly and reversibly.
misconceptions: Git never executes repository-controlled programs until after the user explicitly runs a build.
masteryEvidence: A changed authentic task independently demonstrates this outcome: Inspect untrusted repositories as data in an isolated environment; disable execution paths, verify objects and config, and grant trust narrowly and reversibly.

competencies.statement: Explain gc, repack, cruft packs, prune, expiration, concurrent writers, keep files, geometric repack, and why manual cleanup needs reachability proof.
misconceptions: git gc only compresses reachable data and can never expire recovery objects or race another process.
masteryEvidence: A changed authentic task independently demonstrates this outcome: Explain gc, repack, cruft packs, prune, expiration, concurrent writers, keep files, geometric repack, and why manual cleanup needs reachability proof.

competencies.statement: Configure incremental-repack, loose-objects, commit-graph, prefetch, gc, worktree, rerere-gc, and scheduler strategy from measured need.
misconceptions: Enabling every maintenance task at the shortest interval always improves repository performance.
masteryEvidence: A changed authentic task independently demonstrates this outcome: Configure incremental-repack, loose-objects, commit-graph, prefetch, gc, worktree, rerere-gc, and scheduler strategy from measured need.

competencies.statement: Explain commit-graph generations, changed-path Bloom filters, split chains, multi-pack indexes, bitmaps, and compatibility or invalidation boundaries.
misconceptions: Commit graphs and Bloom filters change reachability truth rather than accelerate selected queries.
masteryEvidence: A changed authentic task independently demonstrates this outcome: Explain commit-graph generations, changed-path Bloom filters, split chains, multi-pack indexes, bitmaps, and compatibility or invalidation boundaries.

competencies.statement: Evaluate built-in fsmonitor, untracked cache, split index, sparse index, preload index, and filesystem support using correctness-first benchmarks.
misconceptions: Filesystem monitoring is a universal correctness layer and never needs platform or invalidation checks.
masteryEvidence: A changed authentic task independently demonstrates this outcome: Evaluate built-in fsmonitor, untracked cache, split index, sparse index, preload index, and filesystem support using correctness-first benchmarks.

competencies.statement: Create full and incremental bundles, declare prerequisites, verify connectivity, restore refs into a fresh repository, and distinguish bundles from complete operational backup.
misconceptions: A successfully created bundle necessarily contains every ref, reflog, hook, config, LFS object, and worktree needed for recovery.
masteryEvidence: A changed authentic task independently demonstrates this outcome: Create full and incremental bundles, declare prerequisites, verify connectivity, restore refs into a fresh repository, and distinguish bundles from complete operational backup.

competencies.statement: Explain SHA-1 and SHA-256 repository object formats, repository initialization choice, ID length, storage identity, and why algorithms are not a cosmetic display option.
misconceptions: Changing objectFormat rewrites only configuration while preserving every existing object ID.
masteryEvidence: A changed authentic task independently demonstrates this outcome: Explain SHA-1 and SHA-256 repository object formats, repository initialization choice, ID length, storage identity, and why algorithms are not a cosmetic display option.

competencies.statement: Evaluate compatibility object formats, translation tables, protocol exchange, clone and fetch interoperability, tooling assumptions, and repository immutability limits.
misconceptions: A compatibility object format lets one repository switch storage hashes back and forth without rewriting objects.
masteryEvidence: A changed authentic task independently demonstrates this outcome: Evaluate compatibility object formats, translation tables, protocol exchange, clone and fetch interoperability, tooling assumptions, and repository immutability limits.

competencies.statement: Inventory scripts, parsers, databases, APIs, hooks, CI, hosting, and documentation for Git 3.0 breaking defaults, long IDs, and version-gated behavior.
misconceptions: If ordinary porcelain commands work, the full toolchain is ready for SHA-256 and Git 3.0.
masteryEvidence: A changed authentic task independently demonstrates this outcome: Inventory scripts, parsers, databases, APIs, hooks, CI, hosting, and documentation for Git 3.0 breaking defaults, long IDs, and version-gated behavior.

competencies.statement: Triage ref, object, pack, index, worktree, remote, credential, and artifact damage; preserve evidence; rebuild from trusted copies; and verify exact losses.
misconceptions: Fetching from origin is a complete repair because remotes necessarily contain every local ref, object, and release artifact.
masteryEvidence: A changed authentic task independently demonstrates this outcome: Triage ref, object, pack, index, worktree, remote, credential, and artifact damage; preserve evidence; rebuild from trusted copies; and verify exact losses.

competencies.statement: Defend repository policy, topology, recovery, scale, trust, release identity, maintenance, migration, drills, residual risks, and owner handoff under changed constraints.
misconceptions: A successful restore drill proves future incidents will have identical data, access, timing, and failure modes.
masteryEvidence: A changed authentic task independently demonstrates this outcome: Defend repository policy, topology, recovery, scale, trust, release identity, maintenance, migration, drills, residual risks, and owner handoff under changed constraints.

credential.title: Advanced Git 2.55: Internals, Recovery, Scale, and Production Workflows Certificate

modules.title: Repository State, References, and Revision Algebra
modules.description: A release repository uses symbolic refs, detached work, replace refs, ambiguous names, and complex revision sets; a maintainer must answer an audit question without moving any ref. Learners cumulatively produce repository-state and revision-query proof through theory, guided work, debugging, transfer, retrieval, and assessment.
objectives: Distinguish direct refs, symbolic refs, pseudorefs, remote-tracking refs, packed refs, and detached HEAD using plumbing evidence.
Resolve revision names and disambiguate refs, object IDs, paths, reflog selectors, and peel operators before an operation.
Construct inclusive, exclusive, symmetric-difference, ancestry, parent, and path-limited revision sets for an exact question.

modules.title: Objects, Packfiles, Reachability, and Integrity
modules.description: A long-lived repository grows unexpectedly, contains unreachable objects, and reports a missing tree after interrupted maintenance; the team needs causal storage evidence before cleanup. Learners cumulatively produce object-database integrity and reachability report through theory, guided work, debugging, transfer, retrieval, and assessment.
objectives: Trace blobs, trees, commits, annotated tags, parent edges, and reference roots as an immutable reachable object graph.
Explain loose objects, pack indexes, delta bases, multi-pack indexes, and why storage representation does not change object identity.
Distinguish reachable, unreachable, dangling, promised, missing, and corrupt objects using fsck and reference evidence.

modules.title: Reflogs, Reachability Windows, and Recovery
modules.description: A force update and expired topic branch hide two weeks of work while automatic maintenance approaches; recovery must preserve candidate objects and establish exact provenance. Learners cumulatively produce reflog-led recovery timeline and retained ref through theory, guided work, debugging, transfer, retrieval, and assessment.
objectives: Explain per-ref and HEAD reflogs, selector syntax, creation policy, worktree scope, expiration categories, and configuration.
Correlate ref updates, checkout history, operation messages, timestamps, actors, and graph evidence without treating reflog order as commit ancestry.
Discover lost tips with reflog, fsck, object inspection, and known content while avoiding commands that may expire or prune candidates.

modules.title: Layered Undo with Restore, Reset, Revert, and References
modules.description: An incident spans unstaged edits, staged policy, three local commits, one published merge, and a release tag; each layer requires a different reversible repair. Learners cumulatively produce layer-by-layer undo decision and verification matrix through theory, guided work, debugging, transfer, retrieval, and assessment.
objectives: Classify the affected worktree, index, ref, graph, published history, and deployed-state layers before selecting an undo operation.
Use restore source, staged, worktree, pathspec, patch, and conflict-stage controls while preserving unrelated changes.
Predict soft, mixed, hard, merge, keep, path-limited, and intent-to-add reset effects on HEAD, index, and worktree.

modules.title: Merge Bases, Topology, and Integration Strategies
modules.description: Two release lines contain criss-cross merges, a reverted integration, rename-heavy work, and policy requiring explainable ancestry; the integrator must choose and defend topology. Learners cumulatively produce merge topology and strategy decision record through theory, guided work, debugging, transfer, retrieval, and assessment.
objectives: Compute one or multiple best common ancestors, octopus bases, fork points, and independent tips for complex graphs.
Choose fast-forward, no-ff, ff-only, or refusal from release policy and graph evidence rather than branch-name folklore.
Evaluate ort strategy behavior and bounded options for renames, whitespace, subtree shifts, and favoring conflict hunks without replacing semantic review.

modules.title: Conflict Stages, Semantic Resolution, and Rerere
modules.description: A repeated long-running integration conflicts across generated code, renamed policy files, and tests; previous manual resolutions are inconsistent and one clean textual result changes behavior. Learners cumulatively produce three-stage conflict and rerere resolution dossier through theory, guided work, debugging, transfer, retrieval, and assessment.
objectives: Inspect stage 1 base, stage 2 ours, stage 3 theirs, unmerged entries, and higher-order conflict metadata before editing.
Distinguish content, add/add, delete/modify, rename, directory/file, binary, mode, submodule, and recursive conflicts and select evidence for each.
Reconstruct intent from base and both histories, produce a semantic combined result, and test invariants that neither side tested alone.

modules.title: Rebase, Sequencer, Autosquash, and Topology Preservation
modules.description: A stacked change series must move between release bases, preserve selected merges, reorder dependent commits, and satisfy a review policy without losing authorship or test evidence. Learners cumulatively produce rebase todo, mapping, and equivalence proof through theory, guided work, debugging, transfer, retrieval, and assessment.
objectives: Model rebase as commit selection, temporary reset, ordered replay, new object creation, and ref movement rather than branch movement magic.
Use --onto, upstream, branch, fork-point, and explicit revision sets to select exactly which commits move and where.
Use pick, reword, edit, squash, fixup, exec, drop, break, label, reset, and merge actions while preserving dependency order.

modules.title: Cherry-Pick, Replay, Patch Equivalence, and Backports
modules.description: A security fix must be backported across three maintained lines where commits differ, one patch is already present under a new ID, and the original series includes a merge. Learners cumulatively produce multi-line backport and patch-equivalence record through theory, guided work, debugging, transfer, retrieval, and assessment.
objectives: Select and order commits for cherry-pick using explicit revision traversal while distinguishing argument order from revision-walk order.
Handle merge mainline, empty, redundant, no-commit, signoff, and conflict continuation choices with release-policy evidence.
Use patch-id, cherry marks, range-diff, and tree comparisons to detect likely equivalent changes while naming heuristic limits.

modules.title: Stash Internals, Partial Work, and Portable Patches
modules.description: An urgent repair interrupts mixed staged, unstaged, untracked, and ignored experiments; some work must remain visible, some move across clones, and no secret may enter an object. Learners cumulatively produce work-in-progress preservation and transfer ledger through theory, guided work, debugging, transfer, retrieval, and assessment.
objectives: Inspect stash commit structure, parents, refs, reflog, staged state, and untracked representation instead of treating a stash as an external bag.
Use pathspec, patch, staged, keep-index, include-untracked, and all controls while previewing secret and ignored-file consequences.
Choose apply, pop, branch, store, create, export, or import from collision risk, portability, and retention needs.

modules.title: Bisect, Reproducible Diagnosis, and Automated Regression Search
modules.description: A nondeterministic performance regression lies within 2,400 commits, skips unbuildable revisions, depends on generated assets, and may have entered on a merged side branch. Learners cumulatively produce reproducible bisect harness and causal report through theory, guided work, debugging, transfer, retrieval, and assessment.
objectives: Explain binary search over candidate ancestry, good and bad polarity, convergence, and the assumptions that make a reported first bad commit meaningful.
Define known good and bad endpoints, path limits, terms, first-parent choices, and ancestry validity before the first trial.
Build a hermetic bisect run harness with explicit exit meanings, timeouts, cleanup, logs, deterministic fixtures, and no unsafe repository code execution.

modules.title: Linked Worktrees and Parallel Branch Operations
modules.description: A maintainer must review a patch, repair production, compare two releases, and build documentation simultaneously without repeated checkout or duplicated object storage. Learners cumulatively produce multi-worktree ownership and cleanup map through theory, guided work, debugging, transfer, retrieval, and assessment.
objectives: Distinguish common repository data from per-worktree HEAD, index, refs, operation state, config, and administrative links.
Add branch, detached, orphan, and relative-path worktrees; then lock, unlock, repair, and move them with path and device constraints.
Reason about checked-out branch exclusivity, shared refs, concurrent fetch or maintenance, and safe cross-worktree branch deletion and rewrite.

modules.title: Remotes, Refspecs, Pruning, and Safe Publication
modules.description: A mirror, fork, upstream, deployment remote, and namespace-specific integration ref disagree after a rewrite; one stale client may overwrite reviewed work. Learners cumulatively produce distributed ref mapping and publication safety plan through theory, guided work, debugging, transfer, retrieval, and assessment.
objectives: Parse positive and negative fetch and push refspecs, wildcard mappings, force markers, deletion, matching, and configured defaults.
Reason about advertised refs, negotiation, shallow or filtered boundaries, tag following, prune, prune-tags, and remote-tracking updates.
Predict fast-forward acceptance by namespace and object type and distinguish local preflight from server receive policy.

modules.title: Stacked Changes, Maintainer Workflows, and Integration Queues
modules.description: A distributed project receives dependent reviews, rerolls, hotfixes, subsystem branches, and release candidates from contributors with different trust and access. Learners cumulatively produce stacked-change and maintainer integration protocol through theory, guided work, debugging, transfer, retrieval, and assessment.
objectives: Represent dependent changes as explicit base and tip relationships, keep each review independently testable, and update downstream stacks after revision.
Use range-diff and interdiff-style evidence to explain rerolls, reordered commits, changed commit messages, and semantic deltas between series.
Compare topic-branch, integration-branch, subsystem, patch-flow, merge-queue, and release-train workflows by authority and recovery cost.

modules.title: Tags, Signatures, Verification, and Release Identity
modules.description: A release tag moved, a lightweight tag shadowed a branch name, signatures use different formats, and an artifact claims a source revision that was never built. Learners cumulatively produce release reference, signature, and artifact provenance packet through theory, guided work, debugging, transfer, retrieval, and assessment.
objectives: Distinguish lightweight refs from annotated tag objects, nested tags, peeling, target types, messages, tagger identity, and replacement risk.
Verify supported GPG, SSH, or X.509 signatures while separating cryptographic validity, key identity, trust policy, authorization, and time evidence.
Compare signed commits, tags, merges, and pushes by what is covered, who verifies them, and which server or client policy applies.

modules.title: Submodules, Nested Repositories, and Dependency Boundaries
modules.description: A product pins three component repositories, one URL moved, a fork rewrites a dependency, recursive checkout executes untrusted configuration, and release builds disagree on nested state. Learners cumulatively produce submodule dependency and trust manifest through theory, guided work, debugging, transfer, retrieval, and assessment.
objectives: Explain gitlink entries, superproject commits, submodule repositories, checked-out commits, .gitmodules, local config, and detached submodule HEAD.
Predict init, update, sync, set-url, set-branch, recursive, remote, merge, rebase, and shallow behavior across configuration layers.
Stage gitlink changes, publish dependency commits before superproject refs, review nested diffs, and verify that every referenced object is reachable.

modules.title: Partial Clone, Sparse Checkout, and Large-Repository Scale
modules.description: A multi-gigabyte monorepo has slow clone and status operations, developers need different cones, CI requires complete release objects, and an unreliable promisor remote may disappear. Learners cumulatively produce large-repository data and working-set contract through theory, guided work, debugging, transfer, retrieval, and assessment.
objectives: Explain promisor remotes, promised objects, filter specifications, lazy fetching, missing-object tolerance, and command compatibility.
Compare shallow clone, partial clone, sparse checkout, sparse index, single branch, and reference repositories by omitted data and semantic limits.
Use cone patterns, non-cone patterns, set, add, reapply, disable, skip-worktree state, and sparse index while detecting vivified paths.

modules.title: Attributes, Filters, Merge Drivers, and Content Policy
modules.description: A cross-platform repository normalizes text, stores generated and binary assets, uses custom merge behavior, and risks data loss through a missing clean filter. Learners cumulatively produce path-attribute and content-transformation policy through theory, guided work, debugging, transfer, retrieval, and assessment.
objectives: Resolve attributes from system, repository, info, directory, pattern, and negative states using check-attr evidence.
Design text, eol, working-tree-encoding, binary, and renormalization policy with byte-level and cross-platform verification.
Configure diff drivers, textconv, binary markers, merge drivers, recursive behavior, and conflict-marker size without treating presentation as storage.

modules.title: Hooks, Configuration, Credentials, and Repository Trust
modules.description: A cloned repository proposes hooks, includeIf config, credential helpers, aliases, filters, safe.directory exceptions, and a maintenance schedule on a shared build host. Learners cumulatively produce repository trust and local automation threat model through theory, guided work, debugging, transfer, retrieval, and assessment.
objectives: Inspect configuration origin, scope, type, includes, conditional includes, worktree config, environment, and command overrides before trusting behavior.
Explain protected configuration and safe.directory ownership exceptions without using broad wildcards to conceal unsafe shared repositories.
Map client and server hook timing, working directory, environment, parameters, exit behavior, bypass paths, distribution, and enforcement limits.

modules.title: Maintenance, Commit Graphs, Index Acceleration, and Bundles
modules.description: A busy monorepo accumulates packs, stale worktrees, slow reachability walks, background jobs, and backup gaps while developers and CI operate concurrently. Learners cumulatively produce measured maintenance, acceleration, and backup schedule through theory, guided work, debugging, transfer, retrieval, and assessment.
objectives: Explain gc, repack, cruft packs, prune, expiration, concurrent writers, keep files, geometric repack, and why manual cleanup needs reachability proof.
Configure incremental-repack, loose-objects, commit-graph, prefetch, gc, worktree, rerere-gc, and scheduler strategy from measured need.
Explain commit-graph generations, changed-path Bloom filters, split chains, multi-pack indexes, bitmaps, and compatibility or invalidation boundaries.

modules.title: Object-Format Transition, Compatibility, and Disaster Defense
modules.description: An organization must prepare for Git 3.0 defaults and SHA-256 repositories while recovering a damaged mirror, validating tooling, and preserving collaboration across object formats. Learners cumulatively produce Git 3.0 readiness and disaster-recovery defense through theory, guided work, debugging, transfer, retrieval, and assessment.
objectives: Explain SHA-1 and SHA-256 repository object formats, repository initialization choice, ID length, storage identity, and why algorithms are not a cosmetic display option.
Evaluate compatibility object formats, translation tables, protocol exchange, clone and fetch interoperability, tooling assumptions, and repository immutability limits.
Inventory scripts, parsers, databases, APIs, hooks, CI, hosting, and documentation for Git 3.0 breaking defaults, long IDs, and version-gated behavior.
